sub.teachers.title: Resources for Teachers
sub.teachers.desc: 344 curated free tools organised by the teaching workflow. Pick a phase, then a topic, to find the right tool.
teachers.picker.label: Explore by Phase
teachers.picker.heading: Choose a phase of the teaching workflow, then a topic
teachers.filter.label: Filter by stage:
teachers.phase.fundamentals.name: Teacher's Fundamentals
teachers.sec.didactics.label: How to Teach
teachers.sec.didactics.desc: Master the craft of teaching: instructional models, lesson structure, content delivery and evidence-based techniques to make any lesson land.
teachers.sec.pedagogy.label: Why You Teach the Way You Do
teachers.sec.pedagogy.h2: Pedagogy — Why You Teach the Way You Do
teachers.sec.pedagogy.desc: Explore the theory and philosophy behind great teaching: learning psychology, motivation, constructivism and the learner's experience.
teachers.sec.together.label: Theory Meets Practice
teachers.sec.together.desc: Resources that bridge research and classroom reality — frameworks, videos of real teaching and toolkits that work because they're grounded in both.
teachers.sec.fund_start.label: Your Starting Point
teachers.sec.fund_start.desc: New to exploring your practice? Start here — a curated path through the most important free resources for developing teachers.
teachers.phase.before.name: Before Class
teachers.sec.planning.label: Curriculum & Lesson Design
teachers.sec.planning.desc: Build standards-aligned units, weekly plans and ready-to-teach lessons in minutes — free templates and full open curricula.
teachers.sec.materials.label: Worksheets, Slides, Video & Visuals
teachers.sec.materials.h2: Creating Materials — Slides, Worksheets, Video & Visuals
teachers.sec.materials.desc: Make polished worksheets, slides, infographics and videos in minutes — no design skills required, all free for teachers.
teachers.sec.adapting.label: Levelling, Accessibility & SEN
teachers.sec.adapting.desc: Pick existing resources and adapt them for every learner — text levellers, leveled libraries, accessibility readers and dyslexia-friendly tools.
teachers.sec.objectives.label: Standards, Frameworks & Lesson Frames
teachers.sec.objectives.desc: Anchor every lesson to clear standards and learning goals — official frameworks, taxonomies and OER alignment libraries.
teachers.phase.during.name: During Class
teachers.sec.explaining.label: Slides, Video & Screencast
teachers.sec.explaining.desc: Deliver lessons that stick — interactive slides, embedded video questions, screencasts and curated mini-lectures.
teachers.sec.interactive.label: STEM Labs, Art & Music Studios
teachers.sec.interactive.h2: Interactive Activities — STEM Labs, Art & Music Studios
teachers.sec.interactive.desc: Hands-on simulations, virtual labs and creative studios — let students DO physics, biology, music and art in your lesson.
teachers.sec.collab.label: Whiteboards, Mind Maps & Brainstorming
teachers.sec.collab.desc: Make student thinking visible — collaborative whiteboards, mind maps, sticky-note boards and concept-mapping tools.
teachers.sec.instruction.label: Polls, Quizzes & Response Systems
teachers.sec.instruction.desc: Run engaging live activities — game-show quizzes, polls, exit tickets and response systems that keep every student active.
teachers.phase.after.name: After Class
teachers.sec.grading.label: LMS, Gradebooks & Auto-grading
teachers.sec.grading.desc: Cut grading time in half — free LMS gradebooks, auto-marked quizzes, rubric tools and standards-based reporting.
teachers.sec.feedback.label: Writing, Grammar & Voice Notes
teachers.sec.feedback.desc: Give meaningful, fast feedback on student writing — grammar checkers, voice notes, rubric tools and writing-help platforms.
teachers.sec.reflection.label: Exit Tickets, Journals & Portfolios
teachers.sec.reflection.desc: Help students think about their learning — video reflections, journals, exit tickets, e-books and portfolios.
teachers.sec.tracking.label: Homework, Practice & Family Learning
teachers.sec.tracking.desc: Recommend safe, effective resources to families — homework support, revision tools and at-home learning for parents.
teachers.phase.supp.name: Supporting
teachers.sec.mgmt.label: Behaviour, Routines & Engagement
teachers.sec.mgmt.desc: Build positive routines, recognise good behaviour, and run smooth daily classroom systems — free tools that respect students.
teachers.sec.comms.label: Parents, Students & Newsletters
teachers.sec.comms.desc: Reach every family in their language and on their terms — secure messaging, newsletters and translation tools.
teachers.sec.admin.label: Cloud Storage, Tasks & Calendars
teachers.sec.admin.desc: Cut down on paperwork — free cloud storage, kanban task boards, scheduling tools and team workspaces.
teachers.sec.wellbeing.label: Mental Health, Mindfulness & Burnout Prevention
teachers.sec.wellbeing.desc: Look after yourself first — free mental-health, mindfulness and burnout-prevention resources made for educators.
teachers.sec.ai.label: AI Assistants for Teachers
teachers.sec.ai.desc: Generate lesson plans, rubrics, differentiated worksheets and feedback in seconds — free AI tools that span the whole workflow.
teachers.phase.furtherdev.name: Further Development
teachers.sec.fd_start.label: New to CPD? Begin Here
teachers.sec.fd_start.h2: Where to Start with Further Development
teachers.sec.fd_start.desc: Not sure where to begin your professional development journey? These trusted starting points will help you plan a path that works for you.
teachers.sec.fd_courses.label: MOOCs, Badges & Certificates
teachers.sec.fd_courses.desc: Earn recognised certificates and badges for free — from Coursera, edX, Google, Microsoft, Apple and the Open University.
teachers.sec.fd_methods.label: PBL, Flipped, Inquiry & More
teachers.sec.fd_methods.h2: Teaching Methods — How to Teach Better
teachers.sec.fd_methods.desc: Explore proven instructional approaches from project-based to flipped learning — free guides, courses and classroom-ready resources.
teachers.sec.fd_subject.label: Deepen Your Subject Knowledge
teachers.sec.fd_subject.desc: Go deeper in your subject area — free resources, enrichment tools and content-specific guidance for maths, literacy, science, humanities and more.
teachers.sec.fd_lead.label: Instructional Coaching & Teacher Leadership
teachers.sec.fd_lead.h2: Lead & Mentor — Become a Lead Teacher
teachers.sec.fd_lead.desc: Move into leadership, coaching and mentoring — free resources for teachers who want to support colleagues and grow their school's practice.
teachers.sec.fd_evidence.label: Research Made Useful for Teachers
teachers.sec.fd_evidence.h2: Evidence & Insights — What the Research Shows
teachers.sec.fd_evidence.desc: Access the best education research in plain English — evidence-rated strategies, key findings and research communities built for busy teachers.
teachers.sec.fd_community.label: Forums, Groups & Professional Networks
teachers.sec.fd_community.desc: Connect with teachers around the world — free forums, social communities, subject networks and live events where great teaching ideas spread.
cd_t_000: The world's most popular learning-science MOOC (4M+ students) — chunking, memory, diffuse thinking. Free to audit.
cd_t_001: Jennifer Gonzalez's podcast and blog — hundreds of free deep-dives on instructional strategies, engagement and classroom technique.
cd_t_002: Six evidence-based study strategies (spaced practice, retrieval, elaboration etc.) explained clearly for teachers and students.
cd_t_003: Vanderbilt's definitive free guide to Bloom's revised taxonomy — verb lists, question stems and lesson-planning templates.
cd_t_004: Free Universal Design for Learning guidelines — plan lessons that work for every learner from the start, not as an afterthought.
cd_t_005: Doug Lemov's 63 classroom techniques — free video clips, read-alongs and field guides on cold calling, pacing and more.
cd_t_006: Research-to-practice articles on differentiation, project-based learning, social-emotional integration and assessment design.
cd_t_007: Free articles on modern pedagogical models — learning taxonomies, innovative frameworks and classroom-ready thinking tools.
cd_t_008: Jim Knight's free resources on the impact cycle — a practical framework any teacher can use for self-directed improvement.
cd_t_009: Free MIT research and tools on teacher learning — simulations, case studies and courses on instructional design.
cd_t_010: Hundreds of free peer-reviewed articles on curriculum, instruction and assessment from the world's leading educator association.
cd_t_011: Free reports translating cognitive science into teacher-prep principles — The Science of Learning made accessible.
cd_t_012: Cognitive scientist's free articles on memory, attention and motivation — translated from research lab to classroom practice.
cd_t_013: Carol Dweck's Mindset Works — free research summaries and classroom-ready strategies for building a growth mindset culture.
cd_t_014: Deci & Ryan's SDT explained — why autonomy, competence and relatedness are the three keys to student motivation.
cd_t_015: Project Zero's free thinking-routine toolkit — See-Think-Wonder, Claim-Support-Question and 20+ classroom-ready protocols.
cd_t_016: Angela Duckworth's free teacher playbooks on grit, curiosity, gratitude and self-control — research-backed, ready to use.
cd_t_017: Berkeley Science Center's free SEL practices grounded in positive psychology — kindness, mindfulness and resilience.
cd_t_018: Free podcast and articles on how learning works — neuroscience, motivation, equity and the future of education.
cd_t_019: Free Harvard Graduate School of Education research articles — equity, learning science, school leadership and policy.
cd_t_020: EEF's free plain-English summary of cognitive load theory — what it means for lesson design, worked examples and practice.
cd_t_021: Clear free guide to Vygotsky's Zone of Proximal Development — how scaffolding and social learning shape the classroom.
cd_t_022: Edutopia's evidence-based synthesis of what the research says makes teaching effective — accessible and practical.
cd_t_023: Harvard PZ's free Visible Thinking project — routines that make student thinking visible, discussable and transferable.
cd_t_024: Video library of real classroom teaching — watch expert teachers in action across subjects and age groups, with reflection guides.
cd_t_025: The gold-standard Project-Based Learning resource — free starter kits, rubrics and project ideas grounded in research.
cd_t_026: IDEO's free design-thinking toolkit for educators — a human-centred process for solving classroom and school challenges.
cd_t_027: Free Expeditionary Learning resources — character, crew and mastery-based assessment grounded in decades of school evidence.
cd_t_028: Free research-to-practice articles with classroom video — each strategy explained, evidenced and shown in action.
cd_t_029: Hattie's 250+ effect sizes with free downloads — understand which teaching practices have the highest impact and why.
cd_t_030: Harvard PZ's free protocols for making student understanding visible — thinking routines with classroom application guides.

cd_t_032: Free-audit courses on designing for learning — environment design, engagement theory and instructional systems thinking.
cd_t_033: Research-backed lesson plans, strategies and classroom management tools — all connected to learning standards.

cd_t_035: Free practical resources from NSF-funded learning-science research — translating neuroscience into classroom action.
